contract_hire.summary: Surepass operates a fixed-term contract hire arrangement, not a daily rental contract. The vehicle is supplied against a New Car Order Form / Vehicle Condition Report, billed monthly in arrears, and an early termination Buy Out Fee applies. A weekly hire rate only takes effect after the contract has expired without renewal.

key_clauses.text: When you sign the 'New Car Order Form' or the 'Vehicle Condition Report and Recurring Contract' you accept the conditions set out in the 'Terms and Conditions of the Car Hire Contract.'
key_clauses.implication: The hire is entered into against a new car order, not a counter rental.

key_clauses.text: You will have the vehicle for the car hire period shown on the New Car Order Form or Vehicle Condition Report.
key_clauses.implication: The hire period is fixed by the order, not chosen day-by-day.

key_clauses.text: The Car Hire Fee shall become due and payable by the last working day of every month.
key_clauses.implication: Monthly billing in arrears — not a pay-now or pay-on-collection model.

key_clauses.text: The Buy Out Fee consists of a standard charge of £300 and £50 charge for every month or part thereof outstanding, up to the expiry date of the Car Hire Contract.
key_clauses.implication: Early termination triggers a buy-out fee, incompatible with a short-term replacement vehicle need.

key_clauses.text: If the hirer cancels a new car which has been ordered, then a £250 cancellation fee will be charged. The fee will also be charged in the event of the new car being cancelled as a result of termination of the contract by either party.
key_clauses.implication: Confirms that a new car must be ordered — the vehicle is not available immediately off a forecourt.

key_clauses.text: Where a contract has expired, and no renewal or termination notice has been received then the car hire charges will revert to a weekly hire rate until the car is returned or a new contract is received.
key_clauses.implication: A weekly rate only operates after contract expiry. The primary commercial basis of the hire is the fixed monthly term.

schedule_1_hirer_responsibilities: Fuel, light bulbs, windscreen wipers and fluid levels between services (Sch 1, item 1).
If the vehicle is returned damaged, an additional £90 hire charge is incurred whilst the vehicle is being repaired (Sch 1, item 5).

Cost of repairs that would normally be covered by warranty but have been refused due to hirer fault — missed services, wrong fuel/oil, low oil, neglect, ignoring warning lights, missed DPF regeneration (Sch 1, item 7).
Surepass recommends brakes are checked at Kwikfit, ATS or R2 Garage every 10,000 miles.
Brake pad replacement covered only while pads are at least 80% worn; brake disc liability limited to one set after 35,000 miles — otherwise the hirer pays.
No-quibble budget tyre replacement, but more than 8 tyres in a 6-month period is treated as wilful abuse and charged to the hirer.

bhr_analysis_notes: NICHE SPECIALIST — NOT MAINSTREAM: Surepass is a driving instruction franchise, not a car rental company. Its vehicles are dual-controlled Vauxhall Corsas designed for driving instructors. It has no presence on mainstream comparison sites and would not be found by a claimant searching for car hire.

NO AVAILABILITY GUARANTEE: Vehicles are primarily used for driving instruction. There is no assurance that a vehicle would be available for a claimant at any given time — availability depends entirely on whether a local franchisee has a spare car not being used for lessons.

DUAL-CONTROLLED VEHICLES — EQUIVALENCE ISSUES: These are specialist instructor vehicles with dual controls. For a non-instructor claimant, a dual-controlled Vauxhall Corsa is not equivalent to a standard rental car of the same or similar group. The dual controls alter the driving experience. For a novice driver/instructor claimant, offering a different type of vehicle risks disrupting lesson quality — particularly close to a driving test date.

LIMITED GEOGRAPHIC COVERAGE: Only 12 collection points across England, each operated by an individual franchisee. Large areas of the UK have no coverage. A claimant would need to live near one of these specific locations to access the service at all.

12-MONTH MINIMUM CONTRACT: The hire agreement is a 12-month minimum with automatic renewal. This is fundamentally incompatible with a short-term replacement vehicle need following an accident. A claimant needing a car for 2-4 weeks of repair would be locked into a year-long contract.

SIGNIFICANT EXIT COSTS: Early termination costs £250 (clause 3.2). Buy-out is £300 plus £50 per month already hired (clause 2.7). These charges would substantially increase the true cost of a short hire period.

RESTRICTIVE USE CONDITIONS: Additional drivers can only drive when the hirer or a driving examiner is present (clause 7.2). This makes the vehicle unsuitable as a general replacement car where family members may also need to drive.
MILEAGE LIMIT: 30,000 miles per year with excess charges. While adequate for most short hires, this is a restriction not typically present in credit hire or mainstream rental.

FRANCHISE OPERATOR — NOT DIRECT RENTAL: The hirer contracts with Surepass centrally but the vehicle is provided by and collected from an individual franchisee (driving instructor). Service standards, vehicle condition, and responsiveness depend on the individual franchisee, not a managed branch network.

DELIVERY & COLLECTION COSTS: D&C is £30 + 80p/mile each way plus VAT. For a claimant not adjacent to a collection point, these charges add significantly to the total cost and are often omitted from BHR rate comparisons.
versions.bhr_rebuttal_notes: Surepass Car Hire is a driving instruction franchise, not a car rental company. Its inclusion in a BHR comparison is fundamentally misconceived for the following reasons: (1) Surepass provides dual-controlled Vauxhall Corsas designed for driving instructors — these are not equivalent to standard rental vehicles and would not meet the reasonable needs of most claimants; (2) There is no guarantee of vehicle availability, as vehicles are primarily used for driving lessons and only available when a local franchisee has a spare car; (3) The 12-month minimum contract with automatic renewal and £250 cancellation / buy-out charges makes it wholly unsuitable for a short-term replacement vehicle need; (4) Only 12 collection points exist across England, each operated by an individual franchisee — coverage is extremely limited; (5) Additional drivers can only operate the vehicle when the hirer or an examiner is present, making it unsuitable as a general replacement car; (6) A novice driver particularly close to a driving test would not want their instructor to offer lessons in a different vehicle type — this raises serious equivalence concerns. The weekly rate of £190 (including VAT and insurance) also excludes delivery and collection charges of £30 + 80p/mile each way plus VAT, which can add substantially to the total cost. The BHR surveyor's reliance on Surepass demonstrates the inadequacy of the comparison exercise — a reasonable person in the claimant's position would not have hired from a driving instruction franchise.

key_contractual_provisions.accident_reporting: Immediately via Driverline 0330 100 8965; written accident report form within 48 hours

key_contractual_provisions.franchise_model: Operations managed by individual dealerships or dealer groups holding the relevant VW Group franchise. Contract is with the specific VWFS Rent-a-Car location, not VWFS centrally.

bhr_analysis_notes: REGIONAL OPERATOR: VWFS Rent-a-Car is a regional operator, not a mainstream national rental company. Per Stevens v Equity, it should be classified as regional for BHR comparison purposes.

FRANCHISE/DEALERSHIP MODEL: Unlike mainstream operators (Avis, Enterprise, Hertz, Europcar) which operate centrally managed branch networks, VWFS operates through individual VW Group dealerships. The rental contract is with the specific location, not with VWFS centrally. This is more akin to a franchise arrangement.

LIMITED GEOGRAPHIC COVERAGE: VWFS locations are tied to VW/Skoda/Seat/Cupra dealerships, not purpose-built rental branches. Coverage is significantly sparser than mainstream operators, with large areas of the UK having no nearby VWFS location.

VEHICLE RANGE: Primarily Volkswagen Group vehicles (VW, Skoda, Seat, Cupra). Does not offer the full range of vehicle groups available from mainstream operators. This limits like-for-like comparisons.

CDW INCLUDED BUT EXCESS HIGH: Standard CDW is included in the rental charge, but the standard excess of £1,000 for cars is high. ECDW to reduce this to £250 is an additional cost not included in the base rate.

SPECIFIC VEHICLE RESERVATIONS: Unlike mainstream operators that reserve by vehicle group, VWFS reserves specific vehicles. This means the quoted rate is for an exact vehicle, not a category — which affects comparability with credit hire rates that are typically quoted by vehicle group.
NO EARLY RETURN REFUND: If a claimant's vehicle is repaired sooner than expected, there is no refund for unused days. This is a cost risk that does not exist with credit hire.

CANCELLATION CHARGES: The 50%/100% cancellation policy for short-notice cancellations penalises claimants whose repair timelines change. Credit hire companies typically do not charge cancellation fees.
MILEAGE LIMITS: Rental includes a mileage allowance with excess charges per mile. Credit hire typically offers unlimited mileage, making the BHR comparison on daily rate alone potentially misleading.
FUEL NOT INCLUDED: Fuel is not included and the refuelling service charge on top of AA pump prices represents an additional cost not reflected in the daily rental rate.
versions.bhr_rebuttal_notes: VWFS Rent-a-Car is a regional operator operating through Volkswagen Group dealerships, not a mainstream national rental company. Its use in BHR comparisons raises several issues: (1) Limited geographic coverage means many claimants would face significant travel to reach a VWFS location, unlike mainstream operators with extensive branch networks; (2) The franchise/dealership model means service standards, vehicle availability, and operational practices vary by location; (3) The standard £1,000 excess for cars requires either self-insurance or purchase of ECDW (reducing to £250) at additional daily cost not reflected in the base rate; (4) Specific vehicle reservations rather than vehicle group bookings limit availability and comparability; (5) No early return refund, strict cancellation charges (50%/100%), and mileage limits all represent cost risks and restrictions that do not apply to credit hire. The advertised daily rate therefore understates the true cost and practical limitations of hiring from VWFS compared to credit hire provision.

bhr_analysis.relevance_to_bhr: EXCESS INSURANCE PROVIDER - NOT A RENTAL COMPANY. Questor provides car hire excess reimbursement insurance, which is directly relevant to BHR arguments because it demonstrates the additional cost burden and risk that retail hirers face when renting vehicles.
key_bhr_arguments: ADDITIONAL COST OF RETAIL HIRE: Retail hirers face excess liability (typically £500-£2,500+) that this product exists to mitigate - at additional cost of £3.62-£6.99/day on top of rental charges. This is a cost credit hire claimants do not bear.

REIMBURSEMENT BASIS ONLY: Policy operates on reimbursement basis - hirer must pay the rental company excess FIRST, then claim back. This requires upfront cash/credit availability that many claimants lack.

£500 CASH PAYMENT EXCLUSION: Any claim over £500 where the excess was paid in cash is excluded. This forces reliance on credit/debit cards, creating a further barrier for those without credit facilities.

CREDIT CARD STRONGLY RECOMMENDED: Questor strongly suggests paying excess with credit card. Many claimants in credit hire situations specifically lack access to credit, which is why they need credit hire in the first place.

COMPLEX CLAIMS PROCESS: 30-day reporting deadline, extensive documentation requirements (rental agreement, damage report, driving licence copies, credit card statements, repair invoices with parts/labour breakdown, police reports) - a significant administrative burden.

EXTENSIVE EXCLUSIONS: 26 general exclusions plus section-specific exclusions create significant gaps in coverage. Interior damage, wear and tear, cleaning fees, consequential loss, accessories, and much more are all excluded.
PRE-PURCHASE REQUIREMENT: Policy must be purchased BEFORE the rental agreement commences - no retroactive cover. Retail hirer must plan and purchase in advance.
VEHICLE RESTRICTIONS: Only covers cars up to 9 seats, max £100,000 value, max 20 years old. No vans, motorhomes, commercial vehicles, motorcycles.

CDW PREREQUISITE: Rental Agreement must already include CDW for excess policy to be valid. If rental company CDW is excluded or not purchased, this policy does not help (unless CDW Optional Extra purchased at additional cost).

LOSS OF USE/DIMINISHMENT OF VALUE: These charges from rental companies are only covered under the Administration Charges section up to £500 - which may be far less than the actual charges levied by rental companies on luxury or higher-group vehicles.

ADMINISTRATION CHARGES CAP £500: The £500 cap on admin charges (which includes Loss of Use and Diminishment of Value) is often insufficient given rental companies commonly charge hundreds per day for Loss of Use.
MINIMUM AGE 21: Excludes drivers aged 17-20 who hold full driving licences.
ANNUAL MULTI TRIP LIMITATIONS: 31-day maximum per rental (62 with paid extension) - insufficient for long-term hire needs. No Business Use coverage on annual policies.
THIRD PARTY CLAIMS DELAY: Where third party involved, Questor requires written confirmation of liability determination from rental company before processing - this can take months.
NO CONSEQUENTIAL LOSS: Consequential loss explicitly excluded - additional accommodation, transport costs, debt-collection fees etc. not covered.
GROSS NEGLIGENCE NOT COVERED: Breach of rental T&Cs invalidates cover - if rental company claims breach, Questor won't pay.
bhr_analysis.bhr_rebuttal_notes: Questor Insurance demonstrates a fundamental truth about retail hire: the advertised daily rental rate is NOT the true cost. Retail hirers face significant excess liability (typically £500-£2,500+), which either they self-insure against or must purchase additional excess insurance at £3.62-£6.99/day. Even with this insurance, coverage operates on a reimbursement basis requiring the hirer to fund the excess upfront, has a £500 cap on administration/loss-of-use charges, excludes claims over £500 paid in cash, requires extensive documentation within 30 days, and contains 26+ exclusions that can void the cover entirely. The existence and pricing of this product should be factored into any BHR comparison - a retail daily rate PLUS excess insurance premium PLUS residual uncovered risk is the true cost comparison against credit hire rates which include fully comprehensive cover with no excess liability to the claimant. Credit hire companies absorb the excess risk, provide vehicles without upfront payment, handle all damage administration, and do not require the claimant to navigate complex insurance claims processes.
